reasoning: The forecast asks whether MTB's close price will exceed its 2025-06-22 close (effectively the close on Friday, June 20, which acts as our baseline of 180.98). M&T Bank has experienced a recent rally since April's 2025 Q1 earnings miss, which saw the stock plummet to around 153 before recovering to the current ~181 range. The stock jumped notably from 174.39 to 180.98 in just three trading days preceding the target price date. Due to normal short-term mean-reversion, particularly after a rapid +3.8% short-term surge with lingering concerns over trimmed Q2 deposit/loan guidance, the probability of remaining strictly above this peak in the very short term (one week) is slightly lower than 0.50. Looking further ahead, as time approaches Q2 earnings on July 16 and subsequent dates, the inherent uncertainty of equity returns increases. Abiding strictly by the instruction that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5', the probabilities gradually adjust upwards towards maximum uncertainty (0.50) as the forecast extends out to six months.
